stem: The daily high temperatures (in °F) for a week were: $62, 65, 68, 70, 71, 73, 78$. What is the range?
explanation: Range is max minus min: $78 - 62 = 16$.

stem: A set of quiz scores has minimum $72$ and maximum $95$. What is the range of the scores?
explanation: Range = max − min = $95 - 72 = 23$.

stem: Data set X: $\{4, 5, 5, 6, 6, 6, 7, 7, 8\}$. Data set Y: $\{1, 3, 5, 6, 6, 7, 9, 11, 13\}$. Both have the same mean and median. Which set has the larger standard deviation?

choices.text: They are equal
Set X
It cannot be determined
Set Y

explanation: Set Y's values are more spread out from the mean (range 12 vs. 4), so its standard deviation is larger.

stem: Which measure of spread describes the difference between the maximum and minimum values of a data set?

choices.text: Standard deviation
Interquartile range
Range
Median

explanation: By definition, the range is max − min. IQR uses quartiles, and standard deviation measures average distance from the mean.

stem: A data set has first quartile $Q_1 = 18$ and third quartile $Q_3 = 31$. What is the interquartile range?
All seven values in a data set are equal to $12$. What is the standard deviation of the data set?
explanation: When every value equals the mean, each deviation is zero, so the standard deviation is $0$.

stem: Class A's test scores: $\{80, 82, 83, 85, 85, 86, 88, 90\}$. Class B's test scores: $\{50, 65, 75, 85, 85, 95, 100, 100\}$. Which class's scores have the greater spread?

choices.text: Both are equal
Class A
Class B
Cannot be determined without the mean

explanation: Class B has a range of $50$ and values far from the center; Class A has a range of $10$ with values clustered tightly.

stem: Which of the following best describes what the interquartile range represents?

choices.text: The distance between the smallest and largest values
The average distance of each value from the mean
The spread of the middle $50\%$ of the data
The value that appears most often

explanation: IQR = $Q_3 - Q_1$, which captures the spread of the central 50% of values.

stem: Two bowling teams have the same mean score of $175$. Team Red's scores range from $170$ to $180$; Team Blue's scores range from $140$ to $210$. Which statement is best supported?

choices.text: Team Red is more consistent because its scores have less spread.
Team Blue is more consistent because it has a wider range.
Both teams are equally consistent because they share a mean.
Team Red has a higher average than Team Blue.

explanation: Lower spread (range $10$ vs. $70$) means scores cluster tightly around the mean — that's what consistency means.

stem: A company records the number of customers per hour. A data entry error adds an extreme outlier of $2{,}000$ customers to one row. Which statistic will be MOST affected by this error?

choices.text: Median
Interquartile range
Mode
Standard deviation

explanation: Standard deviation squares deviations from the mean, so a single extreme outlier massively inflates it; median, IQR, and mode are resistant to outliers.

stem: Each value in a data set is increased by $7$. How do the standard deviation and the range change?

choices.text: Both increase by $7$.
Standard deviation stays the same; range increases by $7$.
Standard deviation increases by $7$; range stays the same.
Both stay the same.

explanation: Adding a constant shifts every value equally, so deviations from the mean and max − min are unchanged.

stem: For a five-number summary, the minimum is $12$, $Q_1 = 18$, median $= 24$, $Q_3 = 34$, and maximum is $58$. What are the range and IQR?

choices.text: Range $= 16$, IQR $= 46$
Range $= 46$, IQR $= 16$
Range $= 46$, IQR $= 12$
Range $= 58$, IQR $= 34$

stem: Two rivers have their daily water levels (in feet) recorded for a month. Both have a mean of $6.2$ ft. River A has standard deviation $0.3$ ft; River B has standard deviation $1.8$ ft. Which conclusion is best supported?

choices.text: River A's daily level is more predictable than River B's.
River B typically carries more water than River A.
River A has more outliers than River B.
The two rivers have the same water level every day.

explanation: A smaller standard deviation means values cluster more tightly around the mean, so River A's level varies less day-to-day.

stem: Every value in a data set is multiplied by $3$. What happens to the standard deviation of the new data set?

choices.text: It stays the same.
It is multiplied by $3$.
It is multiplied by $9$.
It is multiplied by $\sqrt{3}$.

explanation: Multiplying every value by a constant $k$ scales each deviation by $k$, so the standard deviation is also multiplied by $k = 3$.

stem: A teacher reports two sections' final exam results: Section 1 has mean $82$, standard deviation $4$; Section 2 has mean $82$, standard deviation $12$. Which statement is best supported?

choices.text: Section 1's scores are more tightly grouped around $82$ than Section 2's.
Section 1 performed better overall than Section 2.
Section 2 had more students than Section 1.
Section 2 had no students who scored below $82$.

explanation: Both sections share the same mean, but the smaller standard deviation tells us Section 1's scores cluster more closely to that average.

stem: A city records monthly rainfall amounts. Most months have between $2$ and $4$ inches, but one month had $18$ inches due to a hurricane. Compared with the IQR, the standard deviation of this data set will be:

choices.text: Much smaller, because IQR uses all the data.
About the same, because both measure spread.
Much larger, because standard deviation is pulled upward by the hurricane month.
Exactly equal, because one outlier doesn't affect either.

explanation: IQR ignores the extreme $18$-inch month since it lies beyond $Q_3$, but standard deviation squares that large deviation, inflating it substantially.

stem: Consider the data set $\{2, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5, 7, 9\}$. An additional value of $40$ is then added to the data set. Which statement accurately describes the change to the range and the IQR?

choices.text: The range increases significantly, but the IQR changes only slightly.
Both the range and the IQR increase significantly.
The IQR increases significantly, but the range stays the same.
Neither the range nor the IQR changes.

explanation: Adding $40$ jumps the maximum from $9$ to $40$, so range jumps from $7$ to $38$; quartiles shift only slightly because $40$ is a single extreme value at the top, leaving IQR resistant.

stem: Two data sets each contain ten positive integers. Set P: $\{10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10\}$. Set Q: $\{1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 19, 19, 19, 19, 19\}$. Both sets have mean $10$. Which of the following is true?

choices.text: Both sets have the same standard deviation because they share a mean.
Set P has the larger standard deviation because it has more values at the mean.
Set Q has the larger standard deviation because its values are all far from the mean.
Standard deviation cannot be compared without knowing the median.

explanation: In set P every deviation is $0$; in set Q every deviation equals $9$. A larger average squared deviation means a larger standard deviation for Q.

stem: A researcher wants to report a measure of spread for household income in a neighborhood where one family earns $10$ times the median income. Which measure is the BEST choice for describing the typical variability of household income?

choices.text: Range, because it uses the extreme values.
Mean, because it accounts for every value.
Interquartile range, because it is resistant to the outlier.
Standard deviation, because it squares deviations.

explanation: IQR depends only on $Q_1$ and $Q_3$, so one extreme high earner does not distort it; range and standard deviation are both pulled upward by that outlier, and the mean is a center, not a spread.

stem: A data set of $15$ values has standard deviation $5$. A new value equal to the mean of the data set is added, creating a new data set of $16$ values. Which statement about the new standard deviation is correct?

choices.text: It is greater than $5$.
It is exactly equal to $5$.
It is less than $5$.
It is exactly $0$.

explanation: Adding a value equal to the mean leaves the mean unchanged but contributes a deviation of $0$, so the average squared deviation decreases, making the new standard deviation slightly smaller than $5$.
